title: Mirror Image Internet Advances Delivery Services for Current and New Customers
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen vendor profile of Mirror Image Internet, a CDN (content delivery network) service provider, produced in May 2004 and underwritten by Mirror Image. Profiles the company's Content Access Point (CAP) architecture, two customer case studies (Fastclick online advertising; anonymous international toy retailer), and the new Smart Content services platform combining XML-based rules engine with CDN delivery. Aberdeen concludes Mirror Image meets CDN availability/cost-cutting requirements and recommends evaluation of next-generation application delivery capabilities.
importance_rationale: Vendor profile captures CDN market dynamics post-dot-bomb shakeout (40 vendors to fewer than 12); illustrates how CDN survivors pivoted to application delivery.
relevance_rationale: CDN architecture and content delivery patterns documented here (geotargeting, adaptive caching, distributed rules engine) became standard web infrastructure patterns.
prescience_rationale: Correctly forecast that CDN value would move beyond static/streaming content to application delivery; predicted XML rules-based content personalization and adaptive content; all became mainstream CDN features.
